school: School of Divine Love

books.title: Oral prayers & poetry (no independent written work survived)
books.note: Her teachings live through Attar's Tazkirat al-Awliya.

school: Kufan Ascetic School — Student of Abu Hanifa who abandoned jurisprudence for complete spiritual retreat

books.title: Oral spiritual counsel (no extant text)
books.note: Abandoned a career as a leading jurist for absolute renunciation; lived as a hermit in Baghdad.

school: Basran School — Founded the first known Sufi ribat (retreat house) at 'Abbadan on the Persian Gulf

books.title: Oral teachings on zuhd (no extant text)
books.note: Established the first formal Sufi communal retreat — the prototype of the later khanqah.

school: Syrian School / Ascetic-Gnostic

books.title: Aphorisms on gnosis (oral tradition)
books.note: No standalone text; preserved in later Sufi compilations.

school: Khorasani Ascetic School

books.title: Aphorisms on detachment (oral tradition — no extant text)
books.note: No written works; story inspired countless later Sufi narratives.

school: Khorasani-Meccan Ascetic School

books.title: Oral Hadith transmissions and sayings on Zuhd (no written work)
books.note: Sayings preserved in Sufi anthologies.

school: Baghdadi Ascetic School — Teacher of Sari al-Saqati; his tomb in Karkh still visited

books.title: Oral teachings on divine love (no extant text)
books.note: His grave became a site of blessing; Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal reportedly visited it for blessings.

school: Prophetic Household (Ahl al-Bayt) — eighth Imam of Twelver Shia; designated heir of Caliph al-Mamun; buried in Mashhad

books.title: Wikipedia — Ali al-Ridha
books.note: Eighth Shia Imam; designated heir of Caliph al-Mamun; his shrine in Mashhad is the largest mosque in the world by capacity

school: Baghdadi Ascetic School

books.title: Sayings on scrupulous piety (oral tradition)
books.note: Preserved in Sufi biographical literature.

school: Baghdad School / Sober Sufism

books.title: Al-Ri'aya li-huquq Allah (Observance of God's Rights)
books.note: The first systematic manual of Sufi ethics.

school: Khorasan School — one of very few women acknowledged by Bayazid Bastami as his spiritual superior; her teachings were transmitted orally through the Sufi lineages of Nishapur

books.title: No written works — teachings transmitted orally by disciples
books.note: Bayazid al-Bistami called her his spiritual teacher — one of the most remarkable attestations of female authority in Sufi history.

school: Egyptian School / early formulator of Ma'rifa

books.title: Aphorisms on gnosis and Ma'rifa (oral tradition)
books.note: No extant book; teachings preserved in al-Sulami's Tabaqat.

school: Baghdad School / Early Systematic Sufism

books.title: Oral teachings on Tawhid and Love (no extant text)
books.note: Teachings survive through Junayd and biographical sources.

school: Khorasan School / Intoxicated (Sukri) Sufism

books.title: Oral Shathiyat — ecstatic utterances ('Glory be to Me! How great is My Glory!')
books.note: No written work; sayings preserved by al-Sahlaji in Nur al-'Ulum.

school: Prophetic Household (Ahl al-Bayt) — ninth Imam of Twelver Shia; became Imam at age 9; renowned for answers to theological questions

books.title: Wikipedia — Muhammad al-Jawad
books.note: Ninth Shia Imam; became Imam at age 9 — the youngest Imam; renowned for profound theological answers despite his young age

school: Khorasan School / Early Theorist of Sainthood

books.title: Khatm al-Awliya (The Seal of Saints)
books.note: First systematic theology of Islamic sainthood.

school: Baghdad School / Junaydiyya — Sober Sufism

books.title: Rasa'il (Letters on the mystical path)
books.note: Foundational letters defining sober Sufi doctrine; the template for all later Sufi epistles.

school: Baghdad School / Mysticism of Love

books.title: Maqamat al-Qulub (Stations of the Hearts)
books.note: No free digital edition confirmed.

school: Baghdad School — Executed for declaring 'I am the Truth'

books.title: Kitab al-Tawasin (The Book of the Letter T)
books.note: Mystic masterwork written before his execution.

school: Baghdad School — direct student of Junayd and intimate companion of al-Hallaj; executed for refusing to denounce Hallaj, completing the pair of Baghdad's greatest martyrs

books.title: Scattered sayings preserved in al-Qushayri's Risala
books.note: His sayings on love and union are among the most quoted in classical Sufi literature despite no surviving independent work.

school: Baghdad School / Hallajiyya

books.title: Ecstatic aphorisms and poetic fragments (no extant book)
books.note: No independent work; sayings in later Sufi anthologies.

school: Shirazi School
Khorasan School — wrote the Kitab al-Luma', the first comprehensive encyclopedia of Sufism

books.title: Kitab al-Luma' fi'l-Tasawwuf
books.url: ,note:"The Book of Flashes — first great encyclopedia of Sufi doctrine and practice

school: Baghdad School — wrote the foundational manual of Islamic spiritual practice that Al-Ghazali used as a model for the Ihya

books.title: Qut al-Qulub (Nourishment of Hearts)
books.note: Major influence on al-Ghazali's Ihya Ulum al-Din.

school: Khorasan School / Historical-Scholarly Sufism

books.title: Kitab al-Luma' fi'l-Tasawwuf (Book of Flashes in Sufism)
books.note: Oldest surviving systematic manual of Sufism.

school: Khorasan School — the great compiler of early Sufi biography and Quranic commentary; his Tabaqat al-Sufiyya was translated into Persian by Khwaja Abdullah Ansari and became the main source for all later Sufi biographical dictionaries

books.title: Tabaqat al-Sufiyya (Generations of the Sufis)
books.note: The earliest and most authoritative biographical dictionary of Sufi masters — the source from which all later dictionaries drew.

school: Bukhari / Transoxianian School
Khorasan School / Orthodox-Sufi Synthesis

books.title: Al-Risala al-Qushayriyya (Epistle on Sufism)
books.note: The single most important classical manual of Sufi doctrine.

school: Khorasan / Hanbali-Sufi School — Combined strict Hanbali theology with Persian Sufi poetry

books.title: Munajat Nameh (Book of Intimate Prayers)
books.note: Most beloved collection of Persian Sufi prayers — still memorised and recited widely.

books.title: Wikipedia — Khwaja Abdullah Ansari
books.note: Wikipedia article

school: Ghaznavid / Lahori School / Chishti lineage

books.title: Kashf al-Mahjub (Unveiling of the Hidden)
books.note: Oldest surviving Persian prose treatise on Sufism. Full English translation free on Project Gutenberg.

books.title: Kashf al-Mahjub (Revelation of the Veiled)
books.note: Oldest Persian Sufi treatise — English

school: Baghdad / Khorasan — Integrative Sober Sufism & Kalam

books.title: Ihya Ulum al-Din (Revival of the Religious Sciences)
books.note: Described as 'If all Islamic books were destroyed, the Ihya alone would suffice.' 4 volumes.

books.title: Wikipedia — Al-Ghazali
books.note: Wikipedia article

books.title: Ihya Ulum al-Din (English)
books.note: Revival of the Religious Sciences — cornerstone of Islamic spirituality

books.title: Deliverance from Error
books.note: Al-Munqidh min al-Dalal — spiritual autobiography

books.title: The Alchemy of Happiness (Kimiya-yi Sa'adat)
books.note: Persian condensation of the Ihya — English translation

books.title: Incoherence of the Philosophers
books.note: Critique of Aristotelian philosophy — English

school: Khorasan School / Love Mysticism

books.title: Sawaneh (Inspirations from the World of Pure Spirits)
books.note: First systematic treatise on love mysticism in Persian literature.

school: Shirazi School — Defended al-Hallaj; master of mystical Quranic commentary; wrote 5,000 visionary experiences

books.title: Abhar al-'Ashiqin (Jasmine of the Lovers)
books.note: Masterpiece of Persian prose on divine love.

books.title: Sharh al-Shathiyyat (Commentary on Ecstatic Sayings of Sufis)

school: Khorasan School — father and first teacher of Jalal ad-Din Rumi; his Ma'arif is a mystical masterpiece

books.title: Ma'arif (Spiritual Insights)
books.url: ,note:"Mystical journal and discourses — the text Rumi grew up reading

school: Waliullahi School — grandson of Shah Waliullah, co-founder of the Tariqa-i-Muhammadiyya with Sayyid Ahmad Barelvi; his Taqwiyat al-Iman became the founding text of Indian Islamic reformism and is still hotly debated

books.title: Taqwiyat al-Iman (Strengthening of Faith)
books.note: The most controversial and influential Islamic reform tract of 19th-century India — condemned by Barelvis, praised by Deobandis.

school: Tariqa-i-Muhammadiyya — student of Shah Abdul Aziz Dehlawi; led the first organised Islamic reform movement in South Asia combining Naqshbandiyya spirituality with active political resistance against Sikh rule; martyred at Balakot 1831

books.title: Sirat al-Mustaqim (The Straight Path)
books.note: Written with Shah Ismail Shaheed — the manifesto of the Tariqa-i-Muhammadiyya reform movement, still influential in South Asian Islam.
